GeoGebra Manual: Circle command can be used with three points to create a circle (circumcircle). Verified at https://geogebra.github.io/docs/manual/en/commands/Circle/

GeoGebra Manual: PerpendicularLine command creates a line through a point perpendicular to a given line. Verified at https://geogebra.github.io/docs/manual/en/commands/PerpendicularLine/. Alternative names (OrthogonalLine) also supported.
GeoGebra basic command for creating lines through two points

pi_items.metadata_value: Task requires construction of Simson Line (perpendicular projections from circumcircle point are collinear)
pi_items.verified_value: Simson Line theorem valid: given triangle ABC and point P on circumcircle, perpendicular projections from P onto the three sides are collinear

privileged_info_summary: The Simson Line construction task uses a valid triangle with vertices A=(0,0), B=(6,0), C=(2,5) that form a well-defined circumcircle with center (3, 1.7) and radius 3.448. The file should be saved as 'simson_line.ggb'. Coordinate tolerance of 0.25 units is used for vertex validation. The three required GeoGebra commands (Circle, PerpendicularLine, Line) are all valid and appropriate for constructing the Simson Line, which is a real geometric theorem where perpendicular projections from a point on the circumcircle onto the triangle sides are collinear.
